title: Whooping Cough Tests: MedlinePlus Medical Test
text:

To avoid getting or spreading whooping cough:
Avoid close contact with those who have whooping cough. 
Stay at home if you or your child has whooping cough. 
Cover your mouth and nose when coughing or sneezing. 
Thoroughly wash your hands, especially before and after handling food or going to the bathroom. 
The infection is usually milder in teens and adults, especially those who have had the vaccine.
What happens during a whooping cough test? 
Two types of tests are commonly used to diagnose whooping cough. Both tests use a sample of fluid and cells from the back of your nose. Your provider may order one or both of these tests:
Bacteria culture test . This test is most accurate when it's done during the first two weeks after coughing begins. Your fluid sample is sent to a lab. Cells in the sample are grown until there are enough to test. The sample is then checked for whooping cough bacteria. Test results may take up to a week. 
PCR (polymerase chain reaction) test . This test is most accurate when it's done in the first three weeks after coughing begins. A PCR test checks your fluid sample for DNA Â left behind by the whooping cough bacteria. Results may be ready in as soon as two hours. 
For a bacteria culture or a PCR test, your provider will use one of these ways to gather a sample of fluid and cells from your nose:
Nasopharyngeal swab test.
